transcript.line: 1Greetings digit 210.So at the end of class
2today on Friday January30th I left us on a

4code. Let me remindyou what we were trying
5to do. I have now fixedit so I'm going to
6show you the graph theway I wanted to plot
7it. Here it is andthis is the number of
8speeches in each locationof the Dan to Dan
9episode that we werelooking at. And as you see,
10this is what I wantedto do. I wanted to
11put the location withthe most speeches at
12the bottom of the graph,and then I could plot
13them in descendingorder. So each one of
14these little wedgesgets smaller and smaller
15based on the numberof speeches. We go 49
16to 33 to 24. so you seehow that works so how
17did I do that andlet me stop the share
18restart it switch to myother desktop and here

20we had been sort wehad been outputting a
21chunk of like a littlebar to stack based on
22these distinct valuesof the where attributes
23on stage and previouslyI had this little
24expath sort what thatdid is sort these
25distinct values the defaultway and expath which
26is alphabetical orderand we decided that
27didn't really look sogood or make that much
28sense so we're gonnado it a different way

30created a new variabledown here that I'm going
31to take out a littleextra white space here
32I'm calling this onesorted stage locations

34I unpack a variable andI go through things,
35this is going to makea series of strings.

49order of the outputbased on the number
50of counts. So thisvalue here is going to
51determine the orderof the output of xsl
52for each. I have tooutput something or
53this doesn't work. SoI say output the value
54of the currentlocation at this point.
55XSL sort is a littlebit of magic, just
56like XPath sort, butthe way it's going
57to work is like everytime the XSL foreach
58runs, it's goingto grab a count
59value, and the outputof this foreach is
60going to be determinedby the sort order,
61which we've now madebased on a number.
